hypothesis: Enhancing selectivity margin by introducing aromatic residues at position 5 and hydrophobic residues at position 11 to improve complementarity with SSTR2-specific ECL2/ECL3/TM5/TM6 regions while avoiding off-target interactions.

critic_feedback_used.hypothesis: The current hypothesis remains valid: SSTR2 selectivity can be achieved by disrupting off-target interactions at non-pharmacophore positions while preserving the pharmacophore. However, the focus must shift to more aggressive mutation of positions 1, 2, 5, 6, 11, and 12 to push Δmargin beyond 0 consistently.

proposed_changes.rationale: These positions are non-pharmacophore and are known to contribute to off-target interactions with SSTR3 and SSTR5. Focusing on these positions will help disrupt conserved pocket contacts while preserving the pharmacophore (positions 7-10 and Cys3/Cys14).
Introducing charged or small hydrophobic residues at these positions can disrupt conserved pocket interactions with off-target SSTRs while preserving the overall scaffold and pharmacophore.
